sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Gregory, has had a non-healing foot ulcer on his right foot for approximately two months.
The ulcer recently became red and started draining.
He reports that it sometimes throbs and feels like it's got some pressure, but no specific pain.
His main concern is the ulcer's redness and drainage.
Past medical history: The patient has diabetes, but his blood sugar is not well-controlled, with an A1c of 10.
He has not checked his blood sugar regularly.
Review of systems: The patient reports that his diabetes is not well-controlled, but no other symptoms are mentioned.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's vital signs are normal, including no fever.
Physical exam: A one by two inch circular wound is present on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot, proximal to the fifth MTP joint.
The wound is red, has drainage, edema, and fluid coming out of it.
There is no necrosis, odor, or bony exposure.
Test Results: An X-ray of the right foot shows no evidence of osteomyelitis.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a diabetic foot ulcer with signs of infection, including redness, drainage, and edema.
Problem list: Diabetic foot ulcer; Infection; Poorly controlled diabetes.
Plan:
I ordered an ABI (ankle brachial index) to determine the blood supply in the patient's foot.
I performed a debridement to remove dead tissue from the ulcer.
I prescribed clindamycin 400mg twice a day for 7 days to treat the infection.
I will obtain a surgical shoe for the patient to wear to reduce pressure on the foot.
The patient will return in two weeks for another debridement and to assess the tissue.
Follow-up: The patient will return in two weeks for a follow-up appointment.
